sample.label: Fibonacci
sample.summary: Iterative and recursive Fibonacci implementations.

samples.sample:
(module (export "fib_iter" (func $fib_iter)) (export "fib_rec" (func $fib_rec))
;; Iterative: loop with two accumulators. (func $fib_iter (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $a i32) (local $b i32) (local $i i32)
(if (i32.lt_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 2)) (then (return (i32.const 1))))
(local.set $a (i32.const 1)) (local.set $b (i32.const 1)) (local.set $i (i32.const 2))
(block $exit (loop $loop (br_if $exit (i32.gt_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n))) (local.set $b (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b))) (local.set $a (i32.sub (local.get $b) (local.get $a))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $b) )
;; Recursive: direct double self-call. (func $fib_rec (param $n i32) (result i32) (if (result i32) (i32.lt_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 2)) (then (i32.const 1)) (else (i32.add (call $fib_rec (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) (call $fib_rec (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 2))) ) ) ) ) )

sample.label: Arithmetic
sample.summary: Small arithmetic helpers with internal calls and select.

samples.sample:
(module (export "add" (func $add)) (export "add3" (func $add3)) (export "add_abs" (func $add_abs))
;; Basic two-operand addition. (func $add (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) )
;; Three-way addition. (func $add3 (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $c i32) (result i32) (i32.add (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) (local.get $c)) )
;; Add absolute values: |a| + |b|. (func $add_abs (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.add (call $abs (local.get $a)) (call $abs (local.get $b))) )
;; Absolute value via select (internal helper). (func $abs (param $v i32) (result i32) (select (local.get $v) (i32.sub (i32.const 0) (local.get $v)) (i32.ge_s (local.get $v) (i32.const 0)) ) ) )

sample.label: Factorial
sample.summary: 32-bit and 64-bit factorial using recursion and loops.

samples.sample:
(module (export "factorial64_rec" (func $factorial64_rec)) (export "factorial64_iter" (func $factorial64_iter)) (export "factorial_rec" (func $factorial_rec)) (export "factorial_iter" (func $factorial_iter))
;; Recursive i64: widens i32 input, returns i64. (func $factorial64_rec (param $n i32) (result i64) (if (result i64) (i32.le_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)) (then (i64.const 1)) (else (i64.mul (i64.extend_i32_s (local.get $n)) (call $factorial64_rec (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) ) ) ) )
;; Iterative i64: counter and accumulator both i64. (func $factorial64_iter (param $n i32) (result i64) (local $count i64) (local $result i64) (local.set $count (i64.extend_i32_s (local.get $n))) (local.set $result (i64.const 1))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i64.le_s (local.get $count) (i64.const 1))) (local.set $result (i64.mul (local.get $result) (local.get $count))) (local.set $count (i64.sub (local.get $count) (i64.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $result) )
;; Recursive: if-expression returning i32. (func $factorial_rec (param $n i32) (result i32) (if (result i32) (i32.le_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)) (then (i32.const 1)) (else (i32.mul (local.get $n) (call $factorial_rec (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) ) ) ) )
;; Iterative: accumulator loop. (func $factorial_iter (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $result i32) (local.set $result (i32.const 1))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.le_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) (local.set $result (i32.mul (local.get $result) (local.get $n))) (local.set $n (i32.sub (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $result) ) )

sample.label: Collatz conjecture
sample.summary: Loop-heavy Collatz steps and peak tracking.

samples.sample:
(module (export "collatz_steps" (func $collatz_steps)) (export "collatz_max" (func $collatz_max))
;; Count steps to reach 1. ;; n -> n/2 if even, 3n+1 if odd. (func $collatz_steps (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $steps i32)
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.le_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) (if (i32.and (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)) (then (local.set $n (i32.add (i32.mul (local.get $n) (i32.const 3)) (i32.const 1)))) (else (local.set $n (i32.shr_u (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))))) (local.set $steps (i32.add (local.get $steps) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $steps) )
;; Highest value reached during the sequence. (func $collatz_max (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $peak i32) (local.set $peak (local.get $n))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.le_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))) (if (i32.and (local.get $n) (i32.const 1)) (then (local.set $n (i32.add (i32.mul (local.get $n) (i32.const 3)) (i32.const 1)))) (else (local.set $n (i32.shr_u (local.get $n) (i32.const 1))))) (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $n) (local.get $peak)) (then (local.set $peak (local.get $n)))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $peak) ) )

sample.label: GCD / LCM
sample.summary: Recursive and iterative Euclidean algorithm plus LCM.

samples.sample:
(module (export "gcd_rec" (func $gcd_rec)) (export "gcd_iter" (func $gcd_iter)) (export "lcm" (func $lcm))
;; Recursive Euclidean algorithm. (func $gcd_rec (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (if (result i32) (i32.eqz (local.get $b)) (then (local.get $a)) (else (call $gcd_rec (local.get $b) (i32.rem_u (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) ) ) ) )
;; Iterative Euclidean algorithm. (func $gcd_iter (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (local $t i32)
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $b))) (local.set $t (i32.rem_u (local.get $a) (local.get $b))) (local.set $a (local.get $b)) (local.set $b (local.get $t)) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $a) )
;; Least common multiple via GCD. (func $lcm (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.div_u (i32.mul (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) (call $gcd_rec (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) ) ) )

sample.label: Select (min / max / clamp)
sample.summary: Branchless min, max, clamp, and abs via wasm select.

samples.sample:
(module (export "min" (func $min)) (export "max" (func $max)) (export "clamp" (func $clamp)) (export "abs" (func $abs))
;; All four use the wasm "select" instruction (ternary).
(func $min (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (select (local.get $a) (local.get $b) (i32.lt_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b))) )
(func $max (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (select (local.get $a) (local.get $b) (i32.gt_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b))) )
;; Clamp: composition of min and max. (func $clamp (param $v i32) (param $lo i32) (param $hi i32) (result i32) (call $min (call $max (local.get $v) (local.get $lo)) (local.get $hi)) )
;; Absolute value via branchless select. (func $abs (param $v i32) (result i32) (select (local.get $v) (i32.sub (i32.const 0) (local.get $v)) (i32.ge_s (local.get $v) (i32.const 0))) ) )

sample.label: Global counter
sample.summary: Mutable global state with simple exported operations.

samples.sample:
(module (global $count (mut i32) (i32.const 0)) (export "increment" (func $increment)) (export "decrement" (func $decrement)) (export "reset" (func $reset)) (export "get_count" (func $get_count))
(func $increment (param $n i32) (global.set $count (i32.add (global.get $count) (local.get $n))) )
(func $decrement (param $n i32) (global.set $count (i32.sub (global.get $count) (local.get $n))) )
(func $reset (global.set $count (i32.const 0)) )
(func $get_count (result i32) (global.get $count) ) )

sample.label: Memory operations
sample.summary: Loads, stores, swaps, and array walking in linear memory.

samples.sample:
(module (memory 1) (export "store_and_load" (func $store_and_load)) (export "swap" (func $swap)) (export "sum_array" (func $sum_array))
;; Store a value and read it back. (func $store_and_load (param $addr i32) (param $val i32) (result i32) (i32.store (local.get $addr) (local.get $val)) (i32.load (local.get $addr)) )
;; Swap two i32 values in memory. (func $swap (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (local $tmp i32) (local.set $tmp (i32.load (local.get $a))) (i32.store (local.get $a) (i32.load (local.get $b))) (i32.store (local.get $b) (local.get $tmp)) )
;; Sum $count consecutive i32 values starting at $ptr. (func $sum_array (param $ptr i32) (param $count i32) (result i32) (local $sum i32) (local $end i32) (local.set $end (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $count) (i32.const 2))))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.ge_u (local.get $ptr) (local.get $end))) (local.set $sum (i32.add (local.get $sum) (i32.load (local.get $ptr)))) (local.set $ptr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.const 4))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $sum) ) )

sample.label: String operations
sample.summary: String-like scans over data segments and byte loads.

samples.sample:
(module (memory 1) (data (offset (i32.const 0)) "Hello, WebAssembly!\00") (export "strlen" (func $strlen)) (export "char_at" (func $char_at)) (export "indexof" (func $indexof))
;; Count bytes until a null terminator. (func $strlen (param $ptr i32) (result i32) (local $len i32)
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.eqz (i32.load8_u (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (local.get $len))))) (local.set $len (i32.add (local.get $len) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $len) )
;; Get byte at index, or -1 if out of bounds. (func $char_at (param $ptr i32) (param $idx i32) (result i32) (if (result i32) (i32.lt_u (local.get $idx) (call $strlen (local.get $ptr))) (then (i32.load8_u (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (local.get $idx)))) (else (i32.const -1)) ) )
;; First index of byte $ch in string, or -1. (func $indexof (param $ptr i32) (param $ch i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (local $c i32)
(block $done (loop $loop (local.set $c (i32.load8_u (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (local.get $i)))) (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $c))) (if (i32.eq (local.get $c) (local.get $ch)) (then (return (local.get $i)))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(i32.const -1) ) )

sample.label: Lookup table
sample.summary: Lookup-table fallback patterns over precomputed data.

samples.sample:
(module (memory 1) ;; Squares 0..9 pre-computed as little-endian i32. (data (offset (i32.const 0)) "\00\00\00\00" ;; 0 "\01\00\00\00" ;; 1 "\04\00\00\00" ;; 4 "\09\00\00\00" ;; 9 "\10\00\00\00" ;; 16 "\19\00\00\00" ;; 25 "\24\00\00\00" ;; 36 "\31\00\00\00" ;; 49 "\40\00\00\00" ;; 64 "\51\00\00\00") ;; 81 (export "square_lookup" (func $square_lookup)) (export "square_compute" (func $square_compute)) (export "is_perfect_square" (func $is_perfect_square))
;; Table lookup for 0..9, fallback to multiply. (func $square_lookup (param $n i32) (result i32) (if (result i32) (i32.and (i32.ge_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 0)) (i32.lt_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 10))) (then (i32.load (i32.shl (local.get $n) (i32.const 2)))) (else (i32.mul (local.get $n) (local.get $n))) ) )
;; Always compute, no lookup. (func $square_compute (param $n i32) (result i32) (i32.mul (local.get $n) (local.get $n)) )
;; Brute-force perfect square check. (func $is_perfect_square (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $i i32)
(if (i32.lt_s (local.get $n) (i32.const 0)) (then (return (i32.const 0))))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.gt_s (i32.mul (local.get $i) (local.get $i)) (local.get $n))) (if (i32.eq (i32.mul (local.get $i) (local.get $i)) (local.get $n)) (then (return (i32.const 1)))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(i32.const 0) ) )

sample.label: Bitwise ops
sample.summary: Rotate, count, and popcount style bit intrinsics.

samples.sample:
(module (export "rotl" (func $rotl)) (export "rotr" (func $rotr)) (export "clz" (func $clz)) (export "ctz" (func $ctz)) (export "popcnt" (func $popcnt))
;; Rotate left. (func $rotl (param $v i32) (param $n i32) (result i32) (i32.rotl (local.get $v) (local.get $n)) )
;; Rotate right. (func $rotr (param $v i32) (param $n i32) (result i32) (i32.rotr (local.get $v) (local.get $n)) )
;; Count leading zeros. (func $clz (param $v i32) (result i32) (i32.clz (local.get $v)) )
;; Count trailing zeros. (func $ctz (param $v i32) (result i32) (i32.ctz (local.get $v)) )
;; Population count (number of 1-bits). (func $popcnt (param $v i32) (result i32) (i32.popcnt (local.get $v)) ) )

sample.label: Hashing (CRC32 / DJB2)
sample.summary: CRC32 and DJB2 hashing with shifts, xor, and tables.

samples.sample:
(module (memory 1) (export "init_crc_table" (func $init_crc_table)) (export "crc32_table" (func $crc32_table)) (export "crc32_bitwise" (func $crc32_bitwise)) (export "djb2" (func $djb2))
;; Build 256-entry CRC32 lookup table at offset 0. ;; Occupies bytes 0..1023 (256 x i32). (func $init_crc_table (local $i i32) (local $j i32) (local $crc i32)
(block $done (loop $outer (br_if $done (i32.ge_u (local.get $i) (i32.const 256))) (local.set $crc (local.get $i)) (local.set $j (i32.const 0))
(block $bit_done (loop $bits (br_if $bit_done (i32.ge_u (local.get $j) (i32.const 8))) (if (i32.and (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1)) (then (local.set $crc (i32.xor (i32.shr_u (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1)) (i32.const 0xEDB88320)))) (else (local.set $crc (i32.shr_u (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1))))) (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $j) (i32.const 1))) (br $bits) ) )
(i32.store (i32.shl (local.get $i) (i32.const 2)) (local.get $crc)) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $outer) ) ) )
;; CRC32 via table lookup (call init_crc_table first). ;; Data buffer must be at offset >= 1024. (func $crc32_table (param $ptr i32) (param $len i32) (result i32) (local $crc i32) (local $end i32)
(local.set $crc (i32.const -1)) (local.set $end (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (local.get $len)))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.ge_u (local.get $ptr) (local.get $end))) (local.set $crc (i32.xor (i32.load (i32.shl (i32.and (i32.xor (local.get $crc) (i32.load8_u (local.get $ptr))) (i32.const 0xFF)) (i32.const 2))) (i32.shr_u (local.get $crc) (i32.const 8)))) (local.set $ptr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(i32.xor (local.get $crc) (i32.const -1)) )
;; CRC32 bitwise — no table needed, 8 iterations per byte. (func $crc32_bitwise (param $ptr i32) (param $len i32) (result i32) (local $crc i32) (local $end i32) (local $j i32)
(local.set $crc (i32.const -1)) (local.set $end (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (local.get $len)))
(block $done (loop $outer (br_if $done (i32.ge_u (local.get $ptr) (local.get $end))) (local.set $crc (i32.xor (local.get $crc) (i32.load8_u (local.get $ptr))))
(local.set $j (i32.const 0)) (block $bit_done (loop $bits (br_if $bit_done (i32.ge_u (local.get $j) (i32.const 8))) (if (i32.and (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1)) (then (local.set $crc (i32.xor (i32.shr_u (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1)) (i32.const 0xEDB88320)))) (else (local.set $crc (i32.shr_u (local.get $crc) (i32.const 1))))) (local.set $j (i32.add (local.get $j) (i32.const 1))) (br $bits) ) )
(local.set $ptr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.const 1))) (br $outer) ) )
(i32.xor (local.get $crc) (i32.const -1)) )
;; DJB2 hash of a null-terminated string. (func $djb2 (param $ptr i32) (result i32) (local $hash i32) (local $c i32) (local.set $hash (i32.const 5381))
(block $done (loop $loop (local.set $c (i32.load8_u (local.get $ptr))) (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $c))) (local.set $hash (i32.add (i32.add (i32.shl (local.get $hash) (i32.const 5)) (local.get $hash)) (local.get $c))) (local.set $ptr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop) ) )
(local.get $hash) ) )

sample.label: Cast-module interception (32-bit)
sample.summary: Full 32-bit signed/unsigned <-> f32/f64 cast matrix via cast-module imports.

samples.sample:
(module ;; Import 32-bit type-cast functions from the "cast" module. ;; wasm2lang intercepts these imports and emits native ;; language casts instead of function calls in output. ;; All 8 combinations of signed/unsigned i32 <-> f32/f64 are exercised. (import "cast" "i32_to_f32" (func $i32_to_f32 (param i32) (result f32))) (import "cast" "i32_to_f64" (func $i32_to_f64 (param i32) (result f64))) (import "cast" "f32_to_i32" (func $f32_to_i32 (param f32) (result i32))) (import "cast" "f64_to_i32" (func $f64_to_i32 (param f64) (result i32))) (import "cast" "u32_to_f32" (func $u32_to_f32 (param i32) (result f32))) (import "cast" "u32_to_f64" (func $u32_to_f64 (param i32) (result f64))) (import "cast" "f32_to_u32" (func $f32_to_u32 (param f32) (result i32))) (import "cast" "f64_to_u32" (func $f64_to_u32 (param f64) (result i32)))
(export "byte_to_unit" (func $byte_to_unit)) (export "unit_to_byte" (func $unit_to_byte)) (export "mix_channels" (func $mix_channels)) (export "round_half_up" (func $round_half_up)) (export "hypotenuse_i32" (func $hypotenuse_i32)) (export "permille" (func $permille))
;; Normalize a byte [0..255] to unit float [0..1]. ;; Exercises u32_to_f32 — byte is treated as unsigned. (func $byte_to_unit (param $b i32) (result f32) (f32.div (call $u32_to_f32 (local.get $b)) (f32.const 255.0)) )
;; Quantize unit float [0..1] back to byte [0..255]. ;; Exercises f32_to_u32 — result is unsigned. (func $unit_to_byte (param $u f32) (result i32) (call $f32_to_u32 (f32.mul (local.get $u) (f32.const 255.0))) )
;; Blend signed channels: a + (b - a) * (t / 255). ;; Exercises i32_to_f32 and f32_to_i32 on signed channel values. (func $mix_channels (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $t i32) (result i32) (call $f32_to_i32 (f32.add (call $i32_to_f32 (local.get $a)) (f32.mul (f32.sub (call $i32_to_f32 (local.get $b)) (call $i32_to_f32 (local.get $a))) (f32.div (call $i32_to_f32 (local.get $t)) (f32.const 255.0))))) )
;; Round a double to nearest signed int, half-up: floor(x + 0.5). ;; Exercises f64_to_i32 on a signed result. (func $round_half_up (param $x f64) (result i32) (call $f64_to_i32 (f64.floor (f64.add (local.get $x) (f64.const 0.5)))) )
;; Integer hypotenuse via f64 sqrt: floor(sqrt(a*a + b*b) + 0.5). ;; Exercises i32_to_f64 and f64_to_i32. (func $hypotenuse_i32 (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (local $da f64) (local $db f64) (local.set $da (call $i32_to_f64 (local.get $a))) (local.set $db (call $i32_to_f64 (local.get $b))) (call $f64_to_i32 (f64.floor (f64.add (f64.sqrt (f64.add (f64.mul (local.get $da) (local.get $da)) (f64.mul (local.get $db) (local.get $db)))) (f64.const 0.5)))) )
;; Unsigned permille: (value / total) * 1000, treating both as unsigned. ;; Exercises u32_to_f64 and f64_to_u32. (func $permille (param $value i32) (param $total i32) (result i32) (call $f64_to_u32 (f64.mul (f64.div (call $u32_to_f64 (local.get $value)) (call $u32_to_f64 (local.get $total))) (f64.const 1000.0))) ) )

sample.label: Cast-module interception (64-bit)
sample.summary: Full 64-bit signed/unsigned <-> f32/f64 cast matrix (JavaScript BigInt / Java long).

samples.sample:
(module ;; Import 64-bit type-cast functions from the "cast" module. ;; wasm2lang intercepts these imports and emits native casts — ;; in JavaScript i64/u64 are BigInt values, in Java they are long. ;; All 8 combinations of signed/unsigned i64 <-> f32/f64 are exercised. (import "cast" "i64_to_f32" (func $i64_to_f32 (param i64) (result f32))) (import "cast" "i64_to_f64" (func $i64_to_f64 (param i64) (result f64))) (import "cast" "f32_to_i64" (func $f32_to_i64 (param f32) (result i64))) (import "cast" "f64_to_i64" (func $f64_to_i64 (param f64) (result i64))) (import "cast" "u64_to_f32" (func $u64_to_f32 (param i64) (result f32))) (import "cast" "u64_to_f64" (func $u64_to_f64 (param i64) (result f64))) (import "cast" "f32_to_u64" (func $f32_to_u64 (param f32) (result i64))) (import "cast" "f64_to_u64" (func $f64_to_u64 (param f64) (result i64)))
(export "micros_to_seconds" (func $micros_to_seconds)) (export "seconds_to_nanos" (func $seconds_to_nanos)) (export "bytes_to_gib_f32" (func $bytes_to_gib_f32)) (export "gib_to_bytes_f32" (func $gib_to_bytes_f32)) (export "scale_u64" (func $scale_u64)) (export "checksum_avg" (func $checksum_avg))
;; Signed microseconds to fractional seconds via f64. ;; Exercises i64_to_f64 on a signed timestamp. (func $micros_to_seconds (param $us i64) (result f64) (f64.div (call $i64_to_f64 (local.get $us)) (f64.const 1000000.0)) )
;; Fractional seconds to signed nanoseconds. ;; Exercises f64_to_i64 on the reverse conversion. (func $seconds_to_nanos (param $s f64) (result i64) (call $f64_to_i64 (f64.mul (local.get $s) (f64.const 1000000000.0))) )
;; Signed bytes to gibibytes via single precision. ;; Exercises i64_to_f32 on a storage-size value. (func $bytes_to_gib_f32 (param $b i64) (result f32) (f32.div (call $i64_to_f32 (local.get $b)) (f32.const 1073741824.0)) )
;; Float gibibytes back to signed bytes. ;; Exercises f32_to_i64 on the reverse conversion. (func $gib_to_bytes_f32 (param $g f32) (result i64) (call $f32_to_i64 (f32.mul (local.get $g) (f32.const 1073741824.0))) )
;; Scale an unsigned 64-bit counter by a double factor. ;; Exercises u64_to_f64 and f64_to_u64. (func $scale_u64 (param $value i64) (param $factor f64) (result i64) (call $f64_to_u64 (f64.mul (call $u64_to_f64 (local.get $value)) (local.get $factor))) )
;; Unsigned checksum average via single-precision float. ;; Exercises u64_to_f32 and f32_to_u64. (func $checksum_avg (param $sum i64) (param $count i64) (result i64) (call $f32_to_u64 (f32.div (call $u64_to_f32 (local.get $sum)) (call $u64_to_f32 (local.get $count)))) ) )

sample.label: Indirect calls (ftable)
sample.summary: Function table dispatch and call_indirect examples.

samples.sample:
(module (memory 1) (type $binop (func (param i32 i32) (result i32))) (table 4 funcref) (elem (i32.const 0) $add $sub $mul $mod)
(export "dispatch" (func $dispatch)) (export "reduce" (func $reduce)) (export "apply_chain" (func $apply_chain))
(func $add (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
(func $sub (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.sub (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
(func $mul (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.mul (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
(func $mod (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (i32.rem_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))
;; Dispatch: call table[op](a, b). ;; op: 0=add, 1=sub, 2=mul, 3=mod. (func $dispatch (param $op i32) (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (result i32) (call_indirect (type $binop) (local.get $a) (local.get $b) (local.get $op)) )
;; Reduce $count values at $ptr using table[op]. ;; Returns accumulated result. (func $reduce (param $op i32) (param $init i32) (param $ptr i32) (param $count i32) (result i32) (local $acc i32) (local $end i32) (local.set $acc (local.get $init)) (local.set $end (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (local.get $count) (i32.const 2))))
(block $done (loop $loop (br_if $done (i32.ge_u (local.get $ptr) (local.get $end))) (local.set $acc (call_indirect (type $binop) (local.get $acc) (i32.load (local.get $ptr)) (local.get $op))) (local.set $ptr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.const 4))) (br $loop)))
(local.get $acc) )
;; Apply a chain of operations: (((seed op0 v0) op1 v1) op2 v2). ;; 3 fixed steps, each with its own op index. (func $apply_chain (param $seed i32) (param $op0 i32) (param $v0 i32) (param $op1 i32) (param $v1 i32) (param $op2 i32) (param $v2 i32) (result i32)
(call_indirect (type $binop) (call_indirect (type $binop) (call_indirect (type $binop) (local.get $seed) (local.get $v0) (local.get $op0)) (local.get $v1) (local.get $op1)) (local.get $v2) (local.get $op2)) ) )

sample.label: SIMD vectors (Java only)
sample.summary: Vector lane operations and arithmetic for Java output.

samples.sample:
(module (export "dot_product" (func $dot_product)) (export "sum_lanes" (func $sum_lanes)) (export "blend" (func $blend)) (export "negate_abs" (func $negate_abs)) (export "shift_and_mask" (func $shift_and_mask))
;; Dot product of two 4-element i32 vectors via SIMD. ;; dot(a,b) = a0*b0 + a1*b1 + a2*b2 + a3*b3 (func $dot_product (param $a0 i32) (param $a1 i32) (param $a2 i32) (param $a3 i32) (param $b0 i32) (param $b1 i32) (param $b2 i32) (param $b3 i32) (result i32) (local $prod v128)
(local.set $prod (i32x4.mul (i32x4.replace_lane 3 (i32x4.replace_lane 2 (i32x4.replace_lane 1 (i32x4.splat (local.get $a0)) (local.get $a1)) (local.get $a2)) (local.get $a3)) (i32x4.replace_lane 3 (i32x4.replace_lane 2 (i32x4.replace_lane 1 (i32x4.splat (local.get $b0)) (local.get $b1)) (local.get $b2)) (local.get $b3))))
(i32.add (i32.add (i32x4.extract_lane 0 (local.get $prod)) (i32x4.extract_lane 1 (local.get $prod))) (i32.add (i32x4.extract_lane 2 (local.get $prod)) (i32x4.extract_lane 3 (local.get $prod)))) )
;; Sum all four lanes of a splatted+shifted vector. ;; Builds [a, a+1, a+2, a+3] then sums. (func $sum_lanes (param $a i32) (result i32) (local $v v128) (local.set $v (i32x4.add (i32x4.splat (local.get $a)) (v128.const i32x4 0 1 2 3)))
(i32.add (i32.add (i32x4.extract_lane 0 (local.get $v)) (i32x4.extract_lane 1 (local.get $v))) (i32.add (i32x4.extract_lane 2 (local.get $v)) (i32x4.extract_lane 3 (local.get $v)))) )
;; Bitwise blend: select bits from a or b per mask. ;; result = (a & mask) | (b & ~mask) (func $blend (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $mask i32) (result i32) (i32x4.extract_lane 0 (v128.bitselect (i32x4.splat (local.get $a)) (i32x4.splat (local.get $b)) (i32x4.splat (local.get $mask)))) )
;; Negate then absolute value: abs(-x). (func $negate_abs (param $x i32) (result i32) (i32x4.extract_lane 0 (i32x4.abs (i32x4.neg (i32x4.splat (local.get $x))))) )
;; Shift left by n, then AND with splat mask. (func $shift_and_mask (param $val i32) (param $n i32) (param $mask i32) (result i32) (i32x4.extract_lane 0 (v128.and (i32x4.shl (i32x4.splat (local.get $val)) (local.get $n)) (i32x4.splat (local.get $mask)))) ) )

sample.label: Switch dispatch (br_table)
sample.summary: Switch-like dispatch recovered from br_table.

samples.sample:
(module (export "eval_rpn_op" (func $eval_rpn_op)) (export "classify" (func $classify))
;; Simple RPN calculator step. ;; op: 0=add, 1=sub, 2=mul, 3=div, 4=mod. ;; Returns result, or 0 for unknown op. (func $eval_rpn_op (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $op i32) (result i32) (block $default (block $mod (block $div (block $mul (block $sub (block $add (br_table $add $sub $mul $div $mod $default (local.get $op))) ;; op 0: add (return (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) ;; op 1: sub (return (i32.sub (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) ;; op 2: mul (return (i32.mul (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) ;; op 3: div (return (i32.div_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) ;; op 4: mod (return (i32.rem_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) ;; default (i32.const 0) )
;; Classify an ASCII character. ;; Returns: 1=digit, 2=uppercase, 3=lowercase, 0=other. (func $classify (param $ch i32) (result i32) (if (i32.and (i32.ge_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 48)) (i32.le_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 57))) (then (return (i32.const 1)))) (if (i32.and (i32.ge_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 65)) (i32.le_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 90))) (then (return (i32.const 2)))) (if (i32.and (i32.ge_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 97)) (i32.le_u (local.get $ch) (i32.const 122))) (then (return (i32.const 3)))) (i32.const 0) ) )

sample.label: Nested blocks (multi-level break)
sample.summary: Multi-level break recovery through nested blocks.

samples.sample:
(module (export "find_2d" (func $find_2d)) (export "early_exit_chain" (func $early_exit_chain))
(memory 1)
;; Search a rows x cols i32 matrix in memory for a value. ;; Returns row * cols + col (flat index), or -1 if not found. ;; Demonstrates nested blocks with br to outer scope. (func $find_2d (param $ptr i32) (param $rows i32) (param $cols i32) (param $needle i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (local $c i32) (local $addr i32)
(block $not_found (block $found (loop $row_loop (br_if $not_found (i32.ge_u (local.get $r) (local.get $rows))) (local.set $c (i32.const 0))
(loop $col_loop (br_if $row_loop (i32.ge_u (local.get $c) (local.get $cols))) (local.set $addr (i32.add (local.get $ptr) (i32.shl (i32.add (i32.mul (local.get $r) (local.get $cols)) (local.get $c)) (i32.const 2)))) ;; Break two levels out on match. (br_if $found (i32.eq (i32.load (local.get $addr)) (local.get $needle))) (local.set $c (i32.add (local.get $c) (i32.const 1))) (br $col_loop))
(local.set $r (i32.add (local.get $r) (i32.const 1))) (br $row_loop)))
;; $found: compute flat index. (return (i32.add (i32.mul (local.get $r) (local.get $cols)) (local.get $c))))
;; $not_found (i32.const -1) )
;; Chain of validation checks — break to $fail on any error. ;; Returns 1 if all pass, 0 otherwise. (func $early_exit_chain (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $c i32) (result i32) (block $fail ;; Check a > 0 (br_if $fail (i32.le_s (local.get $a) (i32.const 0))) ;; Check b in range [1..100] (br_if $fail (i32.lt_s (local.get $b) (i32.const 1))) (br_if $fail (i32.gt_s (local.get $b) (i32.const 100))) ;; Check c != 0 (br_if $fail (i32.eqz (local.get $c))) ;; Check a + b < 200 (br_if $fail (i32.ge_s (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)) (i32.const 200))) ;; All checks passed. (return (i32.const 1))) (i32.const 0) ) )

sample.label: Loop forms (for / while / do-while)
sample.summary: Different structured loop shapes recovered by codegen.

samples.sample:
(module (export "for_count" (func $for_count)) (export "while_count" (func $while_count)) (export "do_while_count" (func $do_while_count)) (export "if_guarded_sum" (func $if_guarded_sum)) (export "multi_guard_sum" (func $multi_guard_sum))
;; Trailing self-continue → for (;;) with leading break guard. ;; Matched as lc$ / lf$ (loopKind 'for') by LoopSimplificationPass. (func $for_count (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (block $exit (loop $loop (br_if $exit (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop))) (local.get $i) )
;; Entry-guard + trailing self-continue, loop immediately enclosed. ;; Rule-1 promotes to lw$ / ly$ (loopKind 'while') → emitted as while (cond). (func $while_count (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (block $exit (loop $loop (br_if $exit (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop))) (local.get $i) )
;; Trailing conditional self-continue, no block wrapper. ;; Matched as le$ (loopKind 'dowhile') → emitted as do { ... } while (cond). (func $do_while_count (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (loop $loop (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br_if $loop (i32.lt_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n)))) (local.get $i) )
;; If-guarded while pattern produced by binaryen:max. ;; Body is a lone if-no-else whose then-arm ends with br $loop. ;; Matched as lw$ / ly$ → still emitted as while (cond). (func $if_guarded_sum (param $n i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (local $sum i32) (loop $loop (if (i32.lt_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n)) (then (local.set $sum (i32.add (local.get $sum) (local.get $i))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop)))) (local.get $sum) )
;; Multiple entry guards at the top of the loop body. ;; Combined into a single while (g0 && g1) by the pass. (func $multi_guard_sum (param $n i32) (param $cap i32) (result i32) (local $i i32) (local $sum i32) (block $exit (loop $loop (br_if $exit (i32.ge_s (local.get $i) (local.get $n))) (br_if $exit (i32.ge_s (local.get $sum) (local.get $cap))) (local.set $sum (i32.add (local.get $sum) (local.get $i))) (local.set $i (i32.add (local.get $i) (i32.const 1))) (br $loop))) (local.get $sum) ) )

sample.label: If-else recovery (chained if-break)
sample.summary: Chained if-break control flow turned back into branches.

samples.sample:
(module (export "sign" (func $sign)) (export "bucket" (func $bucket)) (export "label_kept" (func $label_kept))
;; Single if-then-break + else body. ;; Chain length = 1 → IfElseRecoveryPass elides the enclosing block label ;; and emits a single if / else instead of labeled-break control flow. (func $sign (param $x i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const 1)) (br $done))) (if (i32.lt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const -1)) (br $done))) (local.set $r (i32.const 0))) (local.get $r) )
;; Three chained if-then-break patterns + else body (chain length = 3). ;; Recovered into if / else if / else if / else ladder, label elided. (func $bucket (param $x i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 100)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const 3)) (br $done))) (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 10)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const 2)) (br $done))) (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const 1)) (br $done))) (local.set $r (i32.const 0))) (local.get $r) )
;; Inner then-arm contains a nested br $done, so the block is referenced ;; more than once. Recovery still fires, but the block label is kept. (func $label_kept (param $x i32) (param $y i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0)) (then (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $y) (i32.const 50)) (then (br $done))) (local.set $r (i32.mul (local.get $x) (local.get $y))) (br $done))) (local.set $r (i32.add (local.get $x) (local.get $y)))) (local.get $r) ) )

sample.label: Block guard elision
sample.summary: Guard blocks that collapse into cleaner control flow.

samples.sample:
(module (export "guard_simple" (func $guard_simple)) (export "guard_multi_body" (func $guard_multi_body)) (export "guard_kept" (func $guard_kept))
;; Leading br_if guard as block's first child. ;; BlockGuardElisionPass rewrites (block $L (br_if $L cond) body) ;; into an equivalent if (!cond) { body } — the $L label is no longer ;; referenced and the enclosing block disappears. (func $guard_simple (param $x i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (br_if $done (i32.le_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0))) (local.set $r (local.get $x))) (local.get $r) )
;; Same pattern with multiple body children — still fully elided because ;; the body has no remaining references to $done. (func $guard_multi_body (param $x i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (br_if $done (i32.eqz (local.get $x))) (local.set $r (i32.mul (local.get $x) (i32.const 2))) (local.set $r (i32.add (local.get $r) (i32.const 1)))) (local.get $r) )
;; Body contains a second br_if $done, so the label is still live. ;; Pass only rewrites the leading guard — the inner break keeps a ;; (now named) block, or if unnamed the inner break will target a ;; nearby surviving label. (func $guard_kept (param $x i32) (param $y i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (br_if $done (i32.le_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 0))) (local.set $r (local.get $x)) (br_if $done (i32.gt_s (local.get $y) (i32.const 50))) (local.set $r (i32.mul (local.get $x) (local.get $y)))) (local.get $r) ) )

sample.label: Switch variants (br_table / fallthrough)
sample.summary: Different switch patterns including fallthrough handling.

samples.sample:
(module (export "flat_switch" (func $flat_switch)) (export "requires_label" (func $requires_label)) (export "terminator_dispatch" (func $terminator_dispatch)) (export "non_wrapping_dispatch" (func $non_wrapping_dispatch))
;; Canonical flat switch: nested blocks + br_table at the innermost level. ;; Detected as sw$ — emitted as a native switch (idx) { case 0: ... } ladder. (func $flat_switch (param $idx i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $exit (block $case2 (block $case1 (block $case0 (br_table $case0 $case1 $case2 $exit (local.get $idx))) (local.set $r (i32.const 10)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 20)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 30))) (local.get $r) )
;; Case body contains a conditional break to the outer dispatch block. ;; The switch emission must keep the outer label so the inner break ;; resolves — the emitter marks state.usedLabels for the exit target. (func $requires_label (param $idx i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $exit (block $case2 (block $case1 (block $case0 (br_table $case0 $case1 $case2 $exit (local.get $idx))) (if (i32.eq (local.get $idx) (i32.const 99)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const 77)) (br $exit))) (local.set $r (i32.const 10)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 20)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 30))) (local.get $r) )
;; Each case body ends with return (a terminator) rather than br. ;; The flat-switch detector accepts return / unreachable as valid ;; chain terminators — no synthetic fall-through breaks are inserted. (func $terminator_dispatch (param $a i32) (param $b i32) (param $op i32) (result i32) (block $default (block $mod (block $div (block $mul (block $sub (block $add (br_table $add $sub $mul $div $mod $default (local.get $op))) (return (i32.add (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) (return (i32.sub (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) (return (i32.mul (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) (return (i32.div_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) (return (i32.rem_s (local.get $a) (local.get $b)))) (i32.const 0) )
;; Dispatch block isn't the first child of its parent — it's preceded ;; by `$r = 100`. Detection renames instead of wrapping, and trailing ;; code after the last inner block remains the final case body. (func $non_wrapping_dispatch (param $idx i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (local.set $r (i32.const 100)) (block $exit (block $case2 (block $case1 (block $case0 (br_table $case0 $case1 $case2 $exit (local.get $idx))) (local.set $r (i32.const 10)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 20)) (br $exit)) (local.set $r (i32.const 30))) (local.get $r) ) )

sample.label: State machine (root-switch + epilogue)
sample.summary: Root-switch state machine with shared epilogue blocks.

samples.sample:
(module (export "run" (func $run))
;; Simple state machine driven by br_table inside a loop. The dispatch ;; is the first child of its loop, so it wraps together with the trailing ;; epilogue into a sw$-prefixed block. Epilogue breaks target the outer ;; $done block with the correct depth — the detector preserves this. ;; ;; States: ;; 0: double $x, advance to state 1 ;; 1: decrement $x, advance to state 2 ;; 2: fall through to epilogue; if $x > 50 we store -1 and exit, else ;; bump $x by 25 and restart at state 0. (func $run (param $x i32) (param $state i32) (result i32) (local $r i32) (block $done (loop $loop (block $s2 (block $s1 (block $s0 (br_table $s0 $s1 $s2 $done (local.get $state))) ;; state 0 (local.set $x (i32.mul (local.get $x) (i32.const 2))) (local.set $state (i32.const 1)) (br $loop)) ;; state 1 (local.set $x (i32.sub (local.get $x) (i32.const 1))) (local.set $state (i32.const 2)) (br $loop)) ;; epilogue — runs when state 2 falls through (if (i32.gt_s (local.get $x) (i32.const 50)) (then (local.set $r (i32.const -1)) (br $done))) (local.set $x (i32.add (local.get $x) (i32.const 25))) (local.set $state (i32.const 0)) (br $loop))) (if (i32.eqz (local.get $r)) (then (local.set $r (local.get $x)))) (local.get $r) ) )

sample.label: BigInt i64 ops (JavaScript / Java)
sample.summary: i64-focused examples for BigInt-style JS and Java output.

samples.sample:
(module (export "mix64" (func $mix64)) (export "splitmix64_step" (func $splitmix64_step)) (export "rotl64" (func $rotl64)) (export "popcnt64" (func $popcnt64)) (export "mul_hi_lo" (func $mul_hi_lo))
;; SplitMix64-style avalanche mix. Exercises i64 xor / shr_u / mul with ;; constants that don't fit in i32 — a good showcase of the JavaScript ;; backend's native BigInt arithmetic (vs the asm.js backend, which ;; goes through a pair-of-i32 lowering pipeline). (func $mix64 (param $x i64) (result i64) (local.set $x (i64.mul (i64.xor (local.get $x) (i64.shr_u (local.get $x) (i64.const 30))) (i64.const 0xBF58476D1CE4E5B9))) (local.set $x (i64.mul (i64.xor (local.get $x) (i64.shr_u (local.get $x) (i64.const 27))) (i64.const 0x94D049BB133111EB))) (i64.xor (local.get $x) (i64.shr_u (local.get $x) (i64.const 31))) )
;; SplitMix64 step: advance state by the golden gamma, then mix. (func $splitmix64_step (param $state i64) (result i64) (call $mix64 (i64.add (local.get $state) (i64.const 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15))) )
;; 64-bit rotate left (compiles to i64.rotl then to BigInt ops in JS). (func $rotl64 (param $v i64) (param $n i64) (result i64) (i64.rotl (local.get $v) (local.get $n)) )
;; 64-bit popcount. (func $popcnt64 (param $v i64) (result i64) (i64.popcnt (local.get $v)) )
;; Split a 64-bit product into hi/lo halves. Returns lo via return ;; value and stores hi at memory offset 0 — combined to show both an ;; i64 truncation (wrap_i32) and a shift-and-wrap. (memory 1) (func $mul_hi_lo (param $a i64) (param $b i64) (result i32) (local $p i64) (local.set $p (i64.mul (local.get $a) (local.get $b))) (i32.store (i32.const 0) (i32.wrap_i64 (i64.shr_u (local.get $p) (i64.const 32)))) (i32.wrap_i64 (local.get $p)) ) )

sample.label: Float math (f64)
sample.summary: Floating-point arithmetic and comparisons in f64.

samples.sample:
(module (export "distance" (func $distance)) (export "lerp" (func $lerp)) (export "normalize_angle" (func $normalize_angle)) (export "approx_inv_sqrt" (func $approx_inv_sqrt))
;; Euclidean distance between two 2D points. (func $distance (param $x1 f64) (param $y1 f64) (param $x2 f64) (param $y2 f64) (result f64) (local $dx f64) (local $dy f64) (local.set $dx (f64.sub (local.get $x2) (local.get $x1))) (local.set $dy (f64.sub (local.get $y2) (local.get $y1))) (f64.sqrt (f64.add (f64.mul (local.get $dx) (local.get $dx)) (f64.mul (local.get $dy) (local.get $dy)))) )
;; Linear interpolation: a + (b - a) * t, where t in [0, 1]. (func $lerp (param $a f64) (param $b f64) (param $t f64) (result f64) (f64.add (local.get $a) (f64.mul (f64.sub (local.get $b) (local.get $a)) (local.get $t))) )
;; Normalize angle to [0, 360) degrees. (func $normalize_angle (param $deg f64) (result f64) (local $r f64) (local.set $r (f64.sub (local.get $deg) (f64.mul (f64.floor (f64.div (local.get $deg) (f64.const 360.0))) (f64.const 360.0)))) ;; Handle negative remainder. (if (f64.lt (local.get $r) (f64.const 0.0)) (then (local.set $r (f64.add (local.get $r) (f64.const 360.0))))) (local.get $r) )
;; Newton-Raphson step for inverse square root: 1/sqrt(x). ;; One iteration from initial guess g: g * (1.5 - 0.5*x*g*g). (func $approx_inv_sqrt (param $x f64) (param $guess f64) (result f64) (f64.mul (local.get $guess) (f64.sub (f64.const 1.5) (f64.mul (f64.mul (f64.const 0.5) (local.get $x)) (f64.mul (local.get $guess) (local.get $guess))))) ) )
